oligos.name: CEL-Seq2 primer (barcoded RT primer)
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription / capture primer with T7 promoter, Illumina 5' adapter, UMI, cell barcode and anchored poly(dT)

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter (transcription start at the terminal GGG); GCCGG 5' leader

components.name: Partial Illumina 5' adapter (RA5)
components.role: Shortened Illumina small-RNA 5' adapter; becomes the Read 1 primer landing site

components.name: UMI
components.role: 6-nt unique molecular identifier (NNNNNN), upstream of the barcode

components.name: Cell barcode
components.role: 6-nt sample/cell barcode (Hamming distance >=2); one per well

components.name: Anchor (V)
components.role: 3' anchor base (A/C/G) that seats the primer at the poly(A) junction

oligos.notes: 82-nt primer (shortened from CEL-Seq's 92 nt). 96 barcoded variants are listed in the detailed protocol; e.g. barcode 1 = AGACTC, 4 = AGCTTC, 5 = CATGAG, 46 = TGCAGA. Recommended 10-primer pool: barcodes 1,4,5,9,10,23,25,26,31,46. 168 unique 6-nt barcodes were designed (GC 33-67%, last base != T).

oligos.name: CEL-Seq2 primer 1s (barcode AGACTC)
oligos.notes: NNNNNN = 6-nt UMI; AGACTC = 6-nt cell barcode for well 1. Concrete example of the CEL-Seq2 primer series (1s-96s) transcribed verbatim from the detailed protocol table.

oligos.name: CEL-Seq primer (original, 8-nt barcode)
oligos.role: Original CEL-Seq barcoded RT primer (no UMI)
oligos.notes: Original CEL-Seq design (Table S2); 92 nt, longer T7 promoter and 5' adapter, no UMI.

oligos.name: CEL-Seq + UMI primer (5-nt UMI, 6-nt barcode)
oligos.notes: Table S2; the 'CEL-Seq performed as previously described with a 5-base UMI and 6-base barcode' variant used for comparison.

oligos.name: Library RT primer (randomhexRT)
oligos.role: Random-hexamer RT primer with 5'-tail Illumina 3' adapter; converts fragmented aRNA back to cDNA

components.name: Illumina 3' adapter (RA3, rev-comp orientation)
components.role: 5'-tail matching Illumina small-RNA 3' adapter (rev-comp of TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGC)

components.name: Random hexamer
components.role: Random-priming 3' hexamer that anneals across fragmented aRNA

oligos.notes: CEL-Seq2 change 5: inserts the Illumina 3' adapter at the RT step via a random hexamer, eliminating the ligation step of the original CEL-Seq. Transcribed verbatim from the protocol/Table S2.

oligos.name: RNA PCR Primer (RP1)
oligos.role: Forward library PCR primer; adds P5 and completes the Illumina 5' adapter

components.name: Illumina 5' adapter (RA5)
components.role: Full 5' small-RNA adapter; 3' end anneals to the AGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC tag in the fragment

oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Small-RNA RP1; the documents state 'sequences available from Illumina' and are not printed. Sequence assembled from the verified P5 constant plus the in-document 5' adapter tag.

oligos.name: RNA PCR Index Primer (RPIX)
oligos.role: Reverse indexed library PCR primer; adds i7 sample index and P7 via the Illumina 3' adapter

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: 6-nt sample index (read in the 7-cycle index read)

components.name: Illumina 3' adapter (RA3, rev-comp)
components.role: 3' portion whose 3' end anneals to the RA3 tag added by the library RT primer

oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Small-RNA RPIX; not printed in the docs ('sequences from Illumina kit'). Assembled from the verified P7 constant, a 6-nt index, and the in-document 3' adapter. A uniquely indexed RPIX is added per pooled library.

oligos.name: Illumina 5' small-RNA adapter (RA5)
oligos.role: 5' adapter / Read 1 primer landing region of the final library
oligos.notes: The shortened form AGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC is carried on the CEL-Seq2 primer; full length restored by RP1 during PCR.

oligos.name: Illumina 3' small-RNA adapter (RA3)
oligos.role: 3' adapter / Read 2 primer landing region of the final library
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of the library RT primer 5'-tail (GCCTTGGCACCCGAGAATTCCA).

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina constant; contributed by RP1.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina constant; contributed by RPIX. Appears at the 3' end of the top strand as its reverse complement (ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG).

final_library.source_label: CEL-Seq2 final Illumina small-RNA library (5'->3' top strand)
annotation_lines: GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC = Illumina 5' small-RNA adapter (RA5 / Read 1 primer site)
TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGAACTCCAGTCAC = Illumina 3' small-RNA adapter (RA3 / Read 2 primer site)

evidence.locator: CEL-Seq2 final Illumina small-RNA library (5'->3' top strand)

library_generation.title: Anneal barcoded CEL-Seq2 primer & reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: The barcoded poly(dT) CEL-Seq2 primer captures a single cell's poly(A) mRNA and SuperScript II reverse-transcribes first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: Each cell/well gets a unique 6-nt barcode; the primer also carries a T7 promoter, the Illumina 5' adapter and a 6-nt UMI. SuperScript II extends from the anchored poly(dT) into the transcript body.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis (dsDNA with T7 promoter)
library_generation.summary: RNase H, E. coli DNA Pol I and DNA ligase convert the RNA:cDNA hybrid into double-stranded DNA carrying an intact T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: SuperScript II Double-Stranded cDNA Synthesis Kit reagents. Barcoded samples are then pooled and bead-purified (AMPure XP) before amplification.

library_generation.title: In vitro transcription (T7 linear amplification)
library_generation.summary: T7 RNA polymerase transcribes from the promoter, linearly amplifying each molecule into many antisense aRNA copies.
library_generation.note: IVT (MEGAscript/SuperScript II kit) avoids exponential PCR bias; transcription starts at the terminal GGG so the T7 promoter itself is not copied into the aRNA.

library_generation.title: aRNA fragmentation
library_generation.summary: The amplified RNA is chemically fragmented (Mg2+, heat) to ~200-500 nt.
library_generation.note: Fragmentation is stopped with EDTA and the aRNA is bead-purified (RNAClean XP). Only fragments retaining the 5' adapter + UMI + barcode end carry the demultiplexing information.

library_generation.title: RT of aRNA with random-hexamer / 3'-adapter primer
library_generation.summary: A random hexamer bearing a 5'-tail Illumina 3' adapter reverse-transcribes the fragmented aRNA into cDNA, appending the 3' adapter (ligation-free).
library_generation.note: SuperScript II RT. This CEL-Seq2 change replaces the inefficient adapter ligation of the original CEL-Seq, improving read mapping (93.8% vs 60.9%).

library_generation.title: Library PCR (RP1 + RPIX) — final indexed library
library_generation.summary: Phusion PCR with RP1 (adds P5 + full 5' adapter) and a uniquely indexed RPIX (adds i7 index + P7) produces the sequenceable Illumina small-RNA library.
library_generation.note: 11-15 cycles. Double AMPure XP cleanup; expected 200-400 bp peak. Handled as an Illumina Small-RNA library on HiSeq 2500 rapid mode.

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina small-RNA Read 1 sequencing primer (= RA5 sense; anneals to the bottom strand, extends 5'->3')
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6-nt UMI then the 6-nt cell barcode (UMI is 5' of the barcode in the RT primer), followed by ~3 poly(dT) bases. The barcode demultiplexes wells; the UMI is carried onto the R2 molecule for counting.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATC----------------->   Read 1 primer (= RA5)
5'- GTTCAGAGTTCTACAGTCCGACGATCNNNNNNNNNNNNTTTTTTTTTT...V[cDNA] -3'
3'- CAAGTCTCAAGATGTCAGGCTGCTAGNNNNNNNNNNNNAAAAAAAAAA...B[cDNA] -5'
                              ^^^^^^^^^^^^
                              UMI(6) + cell barcode(6)  (read 5'->3')

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina i7 index sequencing primer (anneals over the P7 region of the top strand, extends 5'->3' toward the insert)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6-nt i7 sample index (7 cycles run). The index is stored as its reverse complement on the top strand, so the read reproduces the designed index sequence.
library_sequencing.diagram:
<------ i7 index read (7 cycles)
                        3'-TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'   i7 index primer
5'- ...GGAACTCCAGTCACNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...CCTTGAGGTCAGTGNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                     ^^^^^^ i7 index (6 nt; stored as rev-comp on top strand)

library_sequencing.primer: Illumina small-RNA Read 2 sequencing primer (= RA3 antisense; anneals to the RA3 region of the top strand, extends 5'->3' into the cDNA)
library_sequencing.note: Reads 36 bases of the 3'-biased cDNA insert (used for genome mapping); paired with the R1 UMI/barcode.
library_sequencing.diagram:
<--------------------------- read (36 nt, into cDNA)
          3'-ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCCTTGAGGTCAGTG-5'   Read 2 primer
5'- ...[cDNA]TGGAATTCTCGGGTGCCAAGGAACTCCAGTCAC[i7]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...[cDNA]ACCTTAAGAGCCCACGGTTCCTTGAGGTCAGTG[i7]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'

title: CEL-Seq2
description: CEL-Seq2 is a 3'-end-tag, early-barcoding single-cell RNA-seq method that linearly amplifies transcripts by in vitro transcription (IVT). A barcoded RT primer carrying a T7 promoter, a shortened Illumina 5' (small-RNA) adapter, a 6-nt UMI and a 6-nt cell barcode captures poly(A) mRNA at the reverse-transcription step. After second-strand synthesis and T7 IVT (linear amplification), the amplified antisense RNA (aRNA) is fragmented, reverse-transcribed with a random hexamer bearing the Illumina 3' adapter, and PCR-amplified with the Illumina small-RNA RP1/RPI primers into a sequenceable library. Read 1 reads the UMI + cell barcode, Read 2 reads the 3'-biased cDNA, enabling accurate molecule counting across many multiplexed cells.

throughput.summary: Highly-multiplexed single cells: 24 fibroblasts (CEL-Seq), 20 (CEL-Seq2), 72 captured on the Fluidigm C1, and dendritic cells in a 384-well plate; 96 barcoded primers per run (168 6-nt barcodes designed).
throughput.cells: Up to 96 cells per plate / C1 run (168 unique barcodes available)

publication.statistical_model: Binomial statistics to convert UMI counts into transcript counts (UMI-based molecule counting; efficiency estimated by linear fit on ERCC spike-in log-log plots)

other.label: RT/detection efficiency
other.value: 19.7% manual, 22% on Fluidigm C1, vs 5.8% for original CEL-Seq (ERCC spike-in estimate)

other.label: Primer length
other.value: Shortened from 92 nt (CEL-Seq) to 82 nt while adding a 6-nt UMI

other.label: Ligation-free prep
other.value: Illumina adapter inserted at RT via random hexamer; raised barcoded-read mapping from 60.9% to 93.8%

other.label: Pipeline
other.value: https://github.com/yanailab/CEL-Seq-pipeline (GPLv3); demultiplex R1 barcode/UMI, Bowtie2 map, UMI-aware htseq-count

data_processing.summary: Read 1 is used to demultiplex the per-well cell barcode and extract the UMI; Read 2 (3'-biased cDNA) is mapped to the genome and reads are assigned to genes, then collapsed by UMI to give a per-cell molecule (UMI) count matrix.
data_processing.statistical_model: Binomial (UMI-based molecule counting; observed UMIs converted to transcript counts, efficiency estimated from ERCC spike-ins)
